lint.command: set -a; [ -f ./.env ] && . ./.env; set +a; cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --bin toggl -- -D warnings

blockingIssues.description: `AuthStatusCommand::get_status()` reports an environment-backed custom session as authenticated whenever `TOGGL_API_TOKEN` is set and `TOGGL_API_URL` exists, but the reviewed commit never validates that URL. A malformed value like `TOGGL_API_URL=fake` is still surfaced as a healthy custom/OpenToggl session, which violates the requirement for actionable provider failures and explicit auth/provider status introspection.

The reviewed feature handoff claimed runtime dotenv autoload was already implemented, but commit 58b4cc9 does not include the startup dotenv call now present here. Because the feature commit lacked the product-code change, the reviewed feature could not deliver the promised direct `cargo run` behavior at its own commit.

Commit 58b4cc9 had no focused regression coverage proving startup-time repo-local `.env` loading. The direct-startup regression test exists only in a later fix commit, so the feature’s cited auth-status coverage would not have caught a missing startup dotenv load.

rejectedObservations.observation: The cli-worker skill now explicitly tells workers to inspect actual diff/behavior before claiming a feature is already implemented, suggesting the earlier procedure was insufficient for this case.

Shared library docs were updated later to state that the binary itself must auto-load `./.env` at startup, which indicates this local-debug rule was important enough to record after the failed review gap.

The `cli-worker` skill says `tests/live_cli.rs` should only be used when real credentials are available, but this feature legitimately needed changes there to make offline milestones safe under fake local-debug credentials.
